headline: iptables / nftables
description: iptables (legacy) and nftables (modern, since kernel 3.13) are Linux firewall frameworks based on Netfilter hooks. nftables replaces iptables with unified syntax, better performance, and IPv4/IPv6 handling. Used to create packet filtering rules (allow/deny traffic).

citation.name: Linux Firewalls: iptables, nftables, and eBPF Security (2025 Edition)
citation.author: multiple

citation.name: Linux Network Security: A 2025 Comprehensive Guide
citation.author: multiple
